title: Amazon Basics Vacuum Compression Zipper Storage Bags with Airtight Valve and Hand Pump, 15-Pack, 2x X-Jumbo, 5x Jumbo, 4x (Large, Medium), 15 count (Pack of 1), Multiple, Clear
features: Set of 15 Compression Bags: 2x X-Jumbo, 5x jumbo, 4x large, 4x medium; ideal for storing off-season clothes, towels, linens and so on; weight of entire item (15 bags): 2kg

Space-Saving: Vacuum-packing storage bags compress clothing, winter clothes, linens, drapery and more down 80%; for compact, stackable storage under bed, in wardrobe, closet, cupboard or in suitcase when travelling

Airtight Storage: Vacuum bags for storage with heavy-duty double-zip seals and triple-seal turbo valve for lasting airtight protection; follow instructions printed on storage bags to ensure for correct sealing when using
Durable: Vacuum seal bags are made with layers of PA and PE films, which improve tear and wear resistance and help protect clothing from moisture, dirt, mould, odours and more
Hand Pump Included: with hand pump included (for use when travelling), valves can also be connected to standard vacuum cleaner hose for faster deflation
Please note: we suggest using a vacuum cleaner to vacuum a Large, Jumbo or X-Jumbo bag for faster results
description: From the manufacturer Vacuum Storage Bags with Hand Pump Our vacuum storage bags provide a simple and convenient solution for vacuum-sealing your seasonal clothing, organizing your living space, and packing for travel. Our bags come in a variety of sizes, allowing you to store away items such as bedding, clothing, blankets, pillows, plush toys, and more, while also saving valuable space in your home How To Use Vacuum Storage Bag Step 1 Open the vacuum bag and put your textiles into it. Leave 4 inch (10.2 cm) space from the closing side. Step 2 Hold one side of the closing edge and firmly slide the zipper clip along the edge for several times. Step 3 Open the cap of the air valve and use a household vacuum cleaner or the included hand pump to vacuum air out Step 4 Tighten the cap to prevent air or dust getting back in. Usage Tips： • Make sure all items are dry before storing them in bags • Fold clothes as neatly and as flatly as possible in order to squeeze more air out • Double check the zip to make sure it is fully sealed and avoid stuff with sharp corners • Do not remove the air valve. Removing will cause the bags to re-inflate • Avoid storing bags near sharp objects or heat sources • Not recommended for leather
